prompt: A clean architectural materials and components display board for a traditional Chinese imperial palace hall, shown from a straight-on orthographic presentation on a light warm neutral background. Arrange all parts neatly in separated groups like a high-end construction inventory sheet, with realistic miniature model rendering, crisp edges, natural shadows, and museum-quality detail. The composition features exactly 7 grouped areas: 1 complete assembled building model in the upper left, 2 large roof assemblies at the upper right, 23 golden roof component pieces beneath them, 4 small roof-corner ornament pieces at the far upper right, 2 wooden roof truss frame assemblies in the mid-right, 6 long wooden beam layers plus 2 thin poles below the trusses, and a lower section containing architectural base and facade parts. The complete assembled building is a symmetrical Chinese palace hall with a golden double-eaved hip roof, red columns, blue-green painted bracket details, and a carved white stone terrace platform with central stairs and side stairs. In the lower-left component area, show 1 detached white stone base platform, 4 upright carved balustrade posts, 1 carved stone ramp panel, and 1 narrow stone stair segment. In the painted woodwork area near the bottom left, show 2 long blue-green-gold crossbeams and 10 blue-green-gold dougong bracket sets arranged in two rows. In the lower middle-right, show exactly 9 red cylindrical columns with pale stone bases. Along the bottom row, show exactly 6 red lacquered door and window units: 1 double palace door, 1 narrow lattice window, 1 wide three-panel lattice window, and 3 additional paired lattice window panels, all with ornate gold medallion details at the base. Use highly detailed carved stone textures, lacquered wood, glazed ceramic roof tiles, and aged timber structure. The overall image should feel like a precise palace construction kit sheet, elegant, symmetrical, isolated, and free of people, labels, text, city background, scaffolding, machinery, or unrelated objects.
